_meta.purpose: PROPOSED content-driven selection layer for a transactional purchase/booking flow (e.g. DB Ticket Kaufprozess). Each section maps a CONTENT SHAPE / INTENT to a concrete section pattern. NOT yet established — patterns/blocks are plan:null stubs pending human capture from Figma. See PROPOSAL.md.

detection.note: PROPOSED detection intents to add to the skill's page-type classifier (currently hardcoded dashboard|landingpage).
detection.whenToClassifyAsCheckout: The prompt describes a purchase / booking / order / application FLOW or a single step of one: selecting a fare/option, entering passenger/customer/payment data, reviewing an order, or confirming a purchase.

_meta.usage: Pick, do not invent. If a content group matches no section, STOP and flag for human review (never approximate). Fill every placeholder with real content; drop a node only when no content applies.
_meta.arrangementGuidelines: Group and arrange sections + their inner elements per context/layout-guidelines.md ('Contentarten', 'Gruppierung', 'Reihenfolgen') and context/gestaltgesetze.md (Ähnlichkeit, Nähe, Prägnanz, gemeinsame Regionen). Equal/comparable content → horizontal; sequential/prioritized content → vertical; responsive fallback to vertical.
_meta.patternSource: Each `pattern` id resolves in checkout/block-patterns.json; each block id in checkout/blocks.json.

progress.intent: Show where the user is in the multi-step flow.
progress.whenToUse: Always, once, as the first section after the Header, when the flow has 2+ steps (e.g. Verbindung → Reisende → Zahlung → Bestätigung). Read-only.
progress.gap: Needs an official DB Stepper/Progress component OR a captured composite block. See PROPOSAL.md gap #1.

selection-list.intent: Let the user choose ONE option from a set of equivalent, priced alternatives.
selection-list.whenToUse: Fare/tariff or option selection (e.g. Sparpreis / Flexpreis / BahnCard). 2–6 equal options, each with title + description + price + a single Radio. Equal-item-action — never one Brand button per option.
selection-list.arrangement: Equal + comparable → arrange HORIZONTALLY (equal-height cards) so options read as equivalent (layout-guidelines.md; Gestalt-Ähnlichkeit). Fall back to a VERTICAL list for >4 options, long per-option copy, or narrow widths.

form-fieldset.intent: Collect grouped user input (passenger data, contact, address, payment details).
form-fieldset.whenToUse: Any group of related fields under a subheading (e.g. 'Reisende', 'Kontaktdaten', 'Zahlungsart'). Uses Input/Select/CustomSelect/Checkbox/Radio/Switch. Repeatable for multiple groups.

order-summary.intent: Show the order/price breakdown so the user can review before paying.
order-summary.whenToUse: A summary of line items + total, optionally with 'Ändern' links. Typically an aside beside the form, or a full-width review before the final confirm step. Read-only except edit Links.

notice.intent: Surface a contextual info / warning / error relevant to the step.
notice.whenToUse: Optional. A single Notification (e.g. 'Nur noch wenige Sparpreise verfügbar', validation errors). Drop when no message applies.

action-bar.intent: Advance or go back in the flow.
action-bar.whenToUse: Always, once, as the last section. A button group: a secondary 'Zurück' (ghost/outlined) next to the single Brand forward action ('Weiter' / 'Zahlungspflichtig bestellen'). ≤1 primary.
